name: W3 Pull Morning Flow
description: 12 min back/lat primer + deeper hip openers. Upgraded from W2: active hangs replace passive, frog stretch and happy baby replace the basic hip work. More intense, more interesting.

exercises.notes: 5 seconds longer than W2. Relax into it, decompress the spine.
More reps than W2. Pull shoulder blades down and together from the hang. Should feel more controlled by now.
NEW — lie on back, knees to one side. 30s each side. Opens thoracic spine and lower back. Different angle from cat-cow.
NEW — 30s each side. From warrior II, forearm to thigh, top arm reaches over. Deep lat stretch + hip opener in one.
NEW — hands and knees, knees wide, sink hips back. INTENSE inner thigh and hip opener. Way different from 90-90. Breathe through it.
NEW — on your back, grab feet, knees toward armpits. Gently rock side to side. Playful but effective hip opener.
30s each side. Keeping this from W2 — still the best hip flexor stretch for desk humans.

tips: 12 min — back primer + deeper hip work. More intense than W2.
Frog stretch is no joke — ease into it. If it's too much, don't go as wide.
Happy baby: looks silly, feels amazing. Rock gently side to side.
If no pull-up bar at home, do the hang/scap work at the gym before your first set
